<L>150552<pc>755,3<k1>BAs<k2>BAs<h>1<e>1
<hom>1.</hom> <s>BAs</s> ¦ <ab>cl.</ab> 1. <ab>Ā.</ab> (<ls>Dhātup. xvi, 23</ls>) <s>BAsate</s> (in older language also <ab>P.</ab> <s>BA/sati</s>, <ls>AV.</ls> &c.; <ab>p.</ab> <s>BA/sat</s>, <ls>RV.</ls>; <ab>pf.</ab> <s>baBAse</s>, <ls>MBh.</ls>; <ab>aor.</ab> <s>aBAsizwa</s> <ab>Gr.</ab>; <ab>fut.</ab> <s>BAsizyate</s>, <s>BAsitA</s>, <ls>ib.</ls>),
<div n="to"/>to shine, be bright, <ls>RV.</ls> &c. &c.;
<div n="to"/>to appear (‘as’ or ‘like’ <ab>nom.</ab> or <ab>instr.</ab> of an abstract noun), occur to the mind, be conceived or imagined, become clear or evident, <ls>Sāh.</ls>; <ls>Vedāntas.</ls> &c.:
<div n="vp"/><ab>Caus.</ab> <s>BAsayati</s>, <s>˚te</s> (<ab>aor.</ab> <s>abaBAsat</s> and <s>abIBasat</s>, <ls>Pāṇ. vii, 4, 3</ls>), to make shine, illuminate, <ls>Up.</ls>; <ls>MBh.</ls> &c. <pb n="756,1"/>;
<div n="to"/>to show, make evident, cause to appear (‘by way of’, <ab>instr.</ab> of an abstract noun), <ls>Bhaṭṭ.</ls>; <ls>Cat.</ls> <ab>Desid.</ab> <s>biBAsizate</s> <ab>Gr.</ab>:
<div n="vp"/><ab>Intens.</ab> <s>bABAsyate</s>, <s>bABAsti</s>, <ls>ib.</ls> (<ab>cf.</ab> √ <s>BA</s>, of which √ <s>BAs</s> is a secondary form). <info westergaard="BAsf,16.23,01.0403"/><info whitneyroots="BAs,110"/><info verb="genuineroot" cp="1Ā,1P"/>
<LEND>

<L>22155<pc>0816-a<k1>BAs<k2>BAs
{#BAs#}¦ {%<ab>f.</ab>%} [{#BAs-BAve kvip#}] {@1@} Light,
lustre, brightness; {#dfSA#} <lbinfo n="niSeMdIvaracA#ruBAsA"/>
{#niSeMdIvaracAruBAsA#} <ls>N. 22. 43</ls>; <ls>R. 9. 21</ls>;  <lbinfo n="ls:Ku.+ 7. 3."/>
<ls>Ku. 7. 3.</ls> {@--2@} A ray of light; <ls>Ki. 5. 38</ls>,
<ls n="Ki. 5.">46</ls>; <ls n="Ki.">9. 6</ls>; <ls>Ratn. 1. 24</ls>; <ls n="Ratn.">4. 16.</ls> {@--3@}
A reflection, an image. {@--4@} Majesty,
glory, splendour. {@--5@} Wish, desire.
{@--Comp.@} {#--karaH#} {1} the sun;  <lbinfo n="ls:Śi. 11.+ 49"/>
<ls>Śi. 11. 49</ls>; <ls>R. 11. 7</ls>; <ls n="R.">12. 25</ls>; <ls>Ku. 6. 49.</ls>
{2} a hero. {3} fire. {4} an epithet
of Śiva. {5} <ab>N.</ab> of a celebrated
Hindu astronomer who is said to
have flourished in the eleventh or
twelfth century <ab>A. D.</ab> {#--(raM)#} gold.
{#˚dyutiH#} N: of Viṣṇu. {#˚priyaH#} a ruby.
{#˚saptamI#} the seventh day in the
bright half of Māgha. {#--kariH#} the
planet Saturn.
<LEND>

<L>4820<pc>204<k1>BAs<k2>BAs

PWK abridges PWG's named apparatus; dropped citations are upstream editorial loss, not a model gap.
MW recomposes the Petersburg dictionaries in English, collapsing named sources to the L. hedge or dropping them.
